notes.anchor: you can be safe through Dolabella
notes.note: P. Cornelius Dolabella, Tullia's husband since 50 BC, had thrown in with Caesar; Cicero reckons his son-in-law's standing might shield the women if they stayed in Rome. The letter was written from Formiae on 22 January 49 BC, days after Caesar crossed the Rubicon.

notes.anchor: Tell Philotimus to see that the house has its ramparts and its guard
notes.note: Philotimus was Terentia's freedman and business agent, here charged with fortifying the household against the disorder of the opening civil war.
